children.label: a
children.text: Definitions and rules of construction set forth in this section are applicable for the purposes of this subchapter.

children.label: b
children.text: The term “person” means any individual, partnership, corporation, trust, estate, cooperative, association, government or governmental subdivision or agency, or other entity.

children.label: c
children.text: The term “consumer” means an individual.

children.label: A
children.text: credit or insurance to be used primarily for personal, family, or household purposes;

children.label: B
children.text: employment purposes; or

children.label: C
children.text: any other purpose authorized under .section 1681b of this title

children.label: i
children.text: report containing information solely as to transactions or experiences between the consumer and the person making the report;

children.label: ii
children.text: communication of that information among persons related by common ownership or affiliated by corporate control; or

children.label: iii
children.text: communication of other information among persons related by common ownership or affiliated by corporate control, if it is clearly and conspicuously disclosed to the consumer that the information may be communicated among such persons and the consumer is given the opportunity, before the time that the information is initially communicated, to direct that such information not be communicated among such persons;

children.label: B
children.text: any authorization or approval of a specific extension of credit directly or indirectly by the issuer of a credit card or similar device;

children.label: C
children.text: any report in which a person who has been requested by a third party to make a specific extension of credit directly or indirectly to a consumer conveys his or her decision with respect to such request, if the third party advises the consumer of the name and address of the person to whom the request was made, and such person makes the disclosures to the consumer required under ; orsection 1681m of this title

children.label: D
children.text: a communication described in subsection () or (x).o1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: medical information;

children.label: B
children.text: an individualized list or description based on the payment transactions of the consumer for medical products or services; or

children.label: C
children.text: an aggregate list of identified consumers based on payment transactions for medical products or services.

children.label: e
children.text: The term “investigative consumer report” means a consumer report or portion thereof in which information on a consumer’s character, general reputation, personal characteristics, or mode of living is obtained through personal interviews with neighbors, friends, or associates of the consumer reported on or with others with whom he is acquainted or who may have knowledge concerning any such items of information. However, such information shall not include specific factual information on a consumer’s credit record obtained directly from a creditor of the consumer or from a consumer reporting agency when such information was obtained directly from a creditor of the consumer or from the consumer.

children.label: f
children.text: The term “consumer reporting agency” means any person which, for monetary fees, dues, or on a cooperative nonprofit basis, regularly engages in whole or in part in the practice of assembling or evaluating consumer credit information or other information on consumers for the purpose of furnishing consumer reports to third parties, and which uses any means or facility of interstate commerce for the purpose of preparing or furnishing consumer reports.

children.label: g
children.text: The term “file”, when used in connection with information on any consumer, means all of the information on that consumer recorded and retained by a consumer reporting agency regardless of how the information is stored.

children.label: h
children.text: The term “employment purposes” when used in connection with a consumer report means a report used for the purpose of evaluating a consumer for employment, promotion, reassignment or retention as an employee.

children.label: A
children.text: the past, present, or future physical, mental, or behavioral health or condition of an individual;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of health care to an individual; or

children.label: C
children.text: the payment for the provision of health care to an individual.2So in original. The period probably should be “; and”.2

does not include the age or gender of a consumer, demographic information about the consumer, including a consumer’s residence address or e-mail address, or any other information about a consumer that does not relate to the physical, mental, or behavioral health or condition of a consumer, including the existence or value of any insurance policy.

children.heading: Definitions Relating to Child Support Obligations.—
children.label: j Definitions Relating to Child Support Obligations.—

children.heading: .—Overdue support
children.label: 1 .—Overdue support
children.text: The term “overdue support” has the meaning given to such term in .section 666(e) of title 42

children.heading: .—State or local child support enforcement agency
children.label: 2 .—State or local child support enforcement agency
children.text: The term “State or local child support enforcement agency” means a State or local agency which administers a State or local program for establishing and enforcing child support obligations.

children.label: A
children.text: has the same meaning as in ; andsection 1691(d)(6) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a denial or cancellation of, an increase in any charge for, or a reduction or other adverse or unfavorable change in the terms of coverage or amount of, any insurance, existing or applied for, in connection with the underwriting of insurance;

children.label: ii
children.text: a denial of employment or any other decision for employment purposes that adversely affects any current or prospective employee;

children.label: iii
children.text: a denial or cancellation of, an increase in any charge for, or any other adverse or unfavorable change in the terms of, any license or benefit described in ; andsection 1681b(a)(3)(D) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: made in connection with an application that was made by, or a transaction that was initiated by, any consumer, or in connection with a review of an account under ; andsection 1681b(a)(3)(F)(ii) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: adverse to the interests of the consumer.

children.heading: .—Applicable findings, decisions, commentary, and orders
children.label: 2 .—Applicable findings, decisions, commentary, and orders
children.text: For purposes of any determination of whether an action is an adverse action under paragraph (1)(A), all appropriate final findings, decisions, commentary, and orders issued under by the Bureau or any court shall apply.section 1691(d)(6) of this title

children.heading: .—Firm Offer of Credit or Insurance
children.label: l .—Firm Offer of Credit or Insurance
A
children.text: before selection of the consumer for the offer; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the purpose of determining whether to extend credit or insurance pursuant to the offer.

children.label: A
children.text: that the consumer continues to meet the specific criteria used to select the consumer for the offer, by using information in a consumer report on the consumer, information in the consumer’s application for the credit or insurance, or other information bearing on the credit worthiness or insurability of the consumer; or

children.label: B
children.text: of the information in the consumer’s application for the credit or insurance, to determine that the consumer meets the specific criteria bearing on credit worthiness or insurability.

children.label: A
children.text: established before selection of the consumer for the offer of credit or insurance; and

children.label: B
children.text: disclosed to the consumer in the offer of credit or insurance.

children.heading: .—Credit or Insurance Transaction That Is Not Initiated by the Consumer
children.label: m .—Credit or Insurance Transaction That Is Not Initiated by the Consumer

children.text: reviewing the account or insurance policy; or
collecting the account.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: n .—State
children.text: The term “State” means any State, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the District of Columbia, and any territory or possession of the United States.
that, but for subsection (d)(2)(D), would be an investigative consumer report;

children.label: A
children.text: procuring an employee for the employer; or

children.label: B
children.text: procuring an opportunity for a natural person to work for the employer;
that is made by a person who regularly performs such procurement;
that is not used by any person for any purpose other than a purpose described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (2); and

children.label: i
children.text: consents orally or in writing to the nature and scope of the communication, before the collection of any information for the purpose of making the communication;

children.label: ii
children.text: consents orally or in writing to the making of the communication to a prospective employer, before the making of the communication; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of consent under clause (i) or (ii) given orally, is provided written confirmation of that consent by the person making the communication, not later than 3 business days after the receipt of the consent by that person;

children.label: B
children.text: the person who makes the communication does not, for the purpose of making the communication, make any inquiry that if made by a prospective employer of the consumer who is the subject of the communication would violate any applicable Federal or State equal employment opportunity law or regulation; and

children.label: i
children.text: discloses in writing to the consumer who is the subject of the communication, not later than 5 business days after receiving any request from the consumer for such disclosure, the nature and substance of all information in the consumer’s file at the time of the request, except that the sources of any information that is acquired solely for use in making the communication and is actually used for no other purpose, need not be disclosed other than under appropriate discovery procedures in any court of competent jurisdiction in which an action is brought; and

children.label: ii
children.text: notifies the consumer who is the subject of the communication, in writing, of the consumer’s right to request the information described in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Consumer Reporting Agency That Compiles and Maintains Files on Consumers on a Nationwide Basis
children.label: p .—Consumer Reporting Agency That Compiles and Maintains Files on Consumers on a Nationwide Basis

children.text: Public record information.
Credit account information from persons who furnish that information regularly and in the ordinary course of business.

children.heading: Definitions Relating to Fraud Alerts.—
children.label: q Definitions Relating to Fraud Alerts.—
A
children.text: is on active duty (as defined in ) or is a reservist performing duty under a call or order to active duty under a provision of law referred to in ; andsection 101(d)(1) of title 10section 101(a)(13) of title 10

children.label: B
children.text: is assigned to service away from the usual duty station of the consumer.

children.label: A
children.text: notifies all prospective users of a consumer report relating to the consumer that the consumer may be a victim of fraud, including identity theft, or is an active duty military consumer, as applicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: is presented in a manner that facilitates a clear and conspicuous view of the statement described in subparagraph (A) by any person requesting such consumer report.

children.heading: .—Identity theft
children.label: 3 .—Identity theft
children.text: The term “identity theft” means a fraud committed using the identifying information of another person, subject to such further definition as the Bureau may prescribe, by regulation.

children.label: A
children.text: that alleges an identity theft;

children.label: B
children.text: that is a copy of an official, valid report filed by a consumer with an appropriate Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency, including the United States Postal Inspection Service, or such other government agency deemed appropriate by the Bureau; and

children.label: C
children.text: the filing of which subjects the person filing the report to criminal penalties relating to the filing of false information if, in fact, the information in the report is false.

children.heading: .—New credit plan
children.label: 5 .—New credit plan
children.text: The term “new credit plan” means a new account under an open end credit plan (as defined in section 1602(i) of this title) or a new credit transaction not under an open end credit plan.1

children.heading: Credit and Debit Related Terms—
children.label: r Credit and Debit Related Terms—
A
children.text: a credit card issuer, in the case of a credit card; and

children.label: B
children.text: a debit card issuer, in the case of a debit card.

children.heading: .—Credit card
children.label: 2 .—Credit card
children.text: The term “credit card” has the same meaning as in .section 1602 of this title

children.heading: .—Debit card
children.label: 3 .—Debit card
children.text: The term “debit card” means any card issued by a financial institution to a consumer for use in initiating an electronic fund transfer from the account of the consumer at such financial institution, for the purpose of transferring money between accounts or obtaining money, property, labor, or services.

children.heading: .—Account and electronic fund transfer
children.label: 4 .—Account and electronic fund transfer
children.text: The terms “account” and “electronic fund transfer” have the same meanings as in .section 1693a of this title

children.heading: .—Credit and creditor
children.label: 5 .—Credit and creditor
children.text: The terms “credit” and “creditor” have the same meanings as in .section 1691a of this title

children.heading: .—Federal Banking Agency
children.label: s .—Federal Banking Agency
children.text: The term “Federal banking agency” has the same meaning as in .section 1813 of title 12

children.heading: .—Financial Institution
children.label: t .—Financial Institution
children.text: The term “financial institution” means a State or National bank, a State or Federal savings and loan association, a mutual savings bank, a State or Federal credit union, or any other person that, directly or indirectly, holds a transaction account (as defined in ) belonging to a consumer.section 461(b) of title 12

assembles and merges information contained in the database of another consumer reporting agency or multiple consumer reporting agencies concerning any consumer for purposes of furnishing such information to any third party, to the extent of such activities; and
does not maintain a database of the assembled or merged information from which new consumer reports are produced.

children.heading: .—Commission
children.label: v .—Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Bureau.3So in original.3

children.label: w
children.text: The term “Bureau” means the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.
medical records or payments;
residential or tenant history;
check writing history;
employment history; or
insurance claims.

children.heading: Exclusion of Certain Communications for Employee Investigations.—
children.label: y Exclusion of Certain Communications for Employee Investigations.—
A
children.text: but for subsection (d)(2)(D), the communication would be a consumer report;

children.label: i
children.text: suspected misconduct relating to employment; or

children.label: ii
children.text: compliance with Federal, State, or local laws and regulations, the rules of a self-regulatory organization, or any preexisting written policies of the employer;

children.label: C
children.text: the communication is not made for the purpose of investigating a consumer’s credit worthiness, credit standing, or credit capacity; and

children.label: i
children.text: to the employer or an agent of the employer;

children.label: ii
children.text: to any Federal or State officer, agency, or department, or any officer, agency, or department of a unit of general local government;

children.label: iii
children.text: to any self-regulatory organization with regulatory authority over the activities of the employer or employee;

children.label: iv
children.text: as otherwise required by law; or

children.label: v
children.text: pursuant to .section 1681f of this title

children.heading: .—Subsequent disclosure
children.label: 2 .—Subsequent disclosure
children.text: After taking any adverse action based in whole or in part on a communication described in paragraph (1), the employer shall disclose to the consumer a summary containing the nature and substance of the communication upon which the adverse action is based, except that the sources of information acquired solely for use in preparing what would be but for subsection (d)(2)(D) an investigative consumer report need not be disclosed.

children.heading: .—Self-regulatory organization defined
children.label: 3 .—Self-regulatory organization defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “self-regulatory organization” includes any self-regulatory organization (as defined in ), any entity established under title I of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 [ et seq.], any board of trade designated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, and any futures association registered with such Commission.section 78c(a)(26) of this title15 U.S.C. 7211

children.heading: .—Veteran
children.label: z .—Veteran
children.text: The term “veteran” has the meaning given the term in .section 101 of title 38

means a medical collection debt of a veteran owed to a non-Department of Veterans Affairs health care provider that was submitted to the Department for payment for health care authorized by the Department of Veterans Affairs; and
includes medical collection debt that the Department of Veterans Affairs has wrongfully charged a veteran.
